name: Zammad Helpdesk REST API
what_it_does: Open-source helpdesk and ticketing system with REST API for managing support tickets, customers, agents, and channels — supporting email, chat, phone, Twitter, and Facebook integrations with full programmatic access via JSON REST API.
use_cases: Creating and updating support tickets programmatically from external systems
Querying ticket status and customer history for agent-driven support automation
Routing tickets to appropriate agents based on rules triggered by API
Integrating Zammad with CI/CD pipelines for automated incident ticket creation
Building self-service customer portals with real-time ticket data via API
not_for: Enterprise ITSM requiring ITIL processes (use ServiceNow or Jira Service Management)
Simple contact forms without full ticketing workflow needs
Teams needing advanced reporting and SLA management out of the box
best_when: A team needs open-source customer support ticketing with full REST API access, self-hosting capability, and multi-channel support without per-agent pricing.
avoid_when: You need enterprise ITSM, complex SLA automation, or teams without technical capacity to self-host.

auth.notes: Multiple auth methods: HTTP Basic, API token, or OAuth2. Token generated per user in Zammad UI. OAuth2 for third-party integrations. Self-hosted — admin controls token management.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source: completely free; Cloud: 3 agents free for 30 days trial
pricing.paid_tiers: Zammad Cloud from $5/agent/month; self-hosted free forever
pricing.notes: GNU AGPL v3 open source — free to self-host. Cloud hosting available from Zammad GmbH. Very cost-effective vs. Zendesk or Freshdesk. German company — strong GDPR compliance.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no limits; Cloud: standard rate limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API comprehensively documented for tickets, users, organizations, and groups. Multiple auth options give flexibility. Webhook support for real-time event notifications. Error responses include human-readable messages.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations idempotent. Ticket creation creates new tickets — no built-in deduplication. Ticket updates are idempotent for same field values. Search queries are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted version requires database setup (PostgreSQL recommended) and maintenance
No official SDK — agents must implement HTTP calls directly
Webhook payload format differs from REST API response format — verify structures
User roles and permissions affect API access — ensure API user has correct role

security_score_components.security_notes: GNU AGPL v3 — fully auditable. TLS enforced on cloud; configurable self-hosted. Multiple auth methods. OAuth2 support. German company with strong GDPR focus. Self-hosted enables full data sovereignty.
